sentence_reviews.text: Quantized inference stores and computes weights or activations in reduced precision, trading numerical fidelity for lower memory, energy, and latency.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: GPTQ and SmoothQuant directly support post-training low-precision weights/activations for LLM inference with reduced memory/computation and accuracy tradeoffs.
sentence_reviews.notes: Directly supported for weights and activations; energy is a reasonable hardware-efficiency implication but less directly evidenced.

sentence_reviews.text: Operational gains introduce new failure modes in concurrency, versioning, security, numerical behavior, observability, and cost. Its direct dependencies include Quantization-aware training & low-precision adaptation, Graph compilers, runtimes & operator fusion.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Quantized inference & compressed deployment is modeled as depending on Quantization-aware training & low-precision adaptation, Graph compilers, runtimes & operator fusion and feeding downstream nodes such as Edge, on-device & neuromorphic deployment, Image generation, editing & design.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
